---
title: "nitrix.geometry.algebra"
generated: true
signature-hash: 5f07bf3ac91ea182
description: "Transform algebra: Lie-group barycentre, geodesic interpolation, fusion."
listing:
  id: nitrix-geometry-algebra-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-geometry-algebra/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.geometry.algebra`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Transform algebra: Lie-group barycentre, geodesic interpolation, fusion.

Beyond the exp/log chart and the pairwise compose / invert operations,
registration needs to *average* and *interpolate* transforms on their manifold
and to *fuse* a multi-stage chain into a single resampling.  The averaging is
the substrate for groupwise / template construction (a template is the Fréchet
mean of the subject transforms), motion summary, and temporal regularisation.

- [`transform_mean`](nitrix-geometry-algebra/transform_mean.qmd) -- the Fréchet (Karcher) mean of homogeneous
  transforms (rigid $SE(n) \subset$ affine), the Riemannian barycentre
  via the log/exp fixed point in the true matrix chart (`matrix_log` /
  `matrix_exp`).
- [`transform_geodesic`](nitrix-geometry-algebra/transform_geodesic.qmd) -- the point at fraction $t$ along the
  geodesic from identity to a transform ($\exp(t \cdot \log T)$; the
  $\tfrac{1}{2}$-point squares to $T$).
- [`velocity_mean`](nitrix-geometry-algebra/velocity_mean.qmd) -- the Fréchet mean of stationary velocity fields, which
  is the (weighted) arithmetic mean (a stationary velocity field *is* its own
  Lie-algebra element).
- [`fuse_transforms`](nitrix-geometry-algebra/fuse_transforms.qmd) -- collapse a multi-stage chain (matrices +
  displacement fields) into one displacement, so the moving image is resampled
  **once** (no compounded interpolation blur; one gather).

The matrix chart (not the closed-form [`rigid_exp`](nitrix-geometry-transform/rigid_exp.qmd) / [`rigid_log`](nitrix-geometry-transform/rigid_log.qmd)) is
used so the mean / geodesic are the *true* group operations: no
$\mathfrak{so}(n)$ log singularity at identity (which the Karcher init
hits), and a genuine geodesic (so that composing the half-way transform with
itself recovers the whole, $T_{1/2} \, T_{1/2} = T$).  Because
`matrix_log` routes through `safe_inv`, the mean and geodesic are
**offline** barycentre / interpolation ops: a healthy GPU runs them jit- and
grad-clean, whereas a machine with a wedged cuSOLVER runs the forward pass
eagerly via the CPU fallback (hence the Python loop, not a scan).
[`fuse_transforms`](nitrix-geometry-algebra/fuse_transforms.qmd) is pure resampling (no `safe_inv`), so it is
GPU-native.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
| `d1` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
| `ndim` | number of spatial dimensions |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-geometry-algebra-symbols}
:::
